events.summary: Joint Land Use Summit agenda item covering long-range planning activities for Danvers. No project specifics, scope, or timeline are provided in the agenda.

Planning Board will present a status update on long-range planning activities at a joint Land Use Summit of the Select Board, Planning Board, and Zoning Board of Appeals. No project specifics or documents are included in the agenda.

Discussion of proposed articles for the 2027 Special Town Meeting at a joint Land Use Summit of the Select Board, Planning Board, and ZBA. The content or number of articles is not specified in the agenda; the land use context suggests zoning or bylaw amendments are likely among them.

Joint board discussion of all potential articles and proposed 2027 Special Town Meeting warrant articles. Content is unknown from the agenda alone but STM articles commonly include zoning bylaw amendments, capital items, and general bylaws.
Agenda item featuring Kim Lundgren Associates presenting on or in connection with the Green Communities program. No project scope, grant amounts, or specific initiatives are described in the agenda.

Kim Lundgren Associates will present to the joint board session on the Green Communities program, which typically involves municipal energy-efficiency designation criteria, grant eligibility, or action-plan compliance. No project specifics are stated in the agenda.
Joint Land Use Summit agenda item presenting a broad overview of all potential town meeting articles. No individual articles, subjects, or sponsors are identified in the agenda.

stories.name: Abiomed R&D Facility Addition — 54 Cherry Hill Drive

events.summary: Request for Determination of Applicability filed with the Danvers Conservation Commission for work at 54 Cherry Hill Drive (DCC File No. 2026-01). Applicant is Abiomed; project scope and relationship to wetland resource areas are not described in the agenda.

Request for Determination of Applicability for expansion of a 10.4-acre R&D facility, replacing approximately 8,000–9,000 sq ft of existing building with a ~23,000 sq ft addition, plus parking expansion, EV charging, ADA routes, and landscaping; net new impervious within the 100-ft buffer is approximately 9,180 sq ft. One commissioner expressed the view that project scale warrants a full Order of Conditions rather than a negative determination. Hearing continued to April 9, 2026 pending a site visit.

Request for Determination of Applicability filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DCC File No. 2026-01) by Abiomed for work at 54 Cherry Hill Drive. The agenda provides no description of the proposed work; outcome of the determination is not yet known.

The Conservation Commission issued a unanimous positive Determination of Applicability for Abiomed's proposed office and parking expansion, requiring the company to refile as a full Notice of Intent before proceeding. The project involves approximately 9,000 sq ft of new pavement, roughly 267 total parking spaces (23–24 new spaces within wetland buffer zones), and a new two-story office building connected to the existing R&D/manufacturing facility; commissioners found the project scope too significant for the RDA process.

Planning Board public hearing on a site plan review application by Abiomed, Inc. for a new building addition, parking lot expansion, and associated site modifications at an Industrial II (I-2) zoned property. No square footage or dollar figures were stated in the agenda.

Notice of Intent filed under 310 CMR 10.05(4) for work at 54 Cherry Hill Drive by a corporate applicant, Abiomed, Inc. (DEP File No. 14-1452). Best-guess type is commercial given the corporate name, but scope and nature of the proposed work are not described in the agenda.

Abiomed, Inc. filed a Notice of Intent for minor site work and installation of a no-storage sign at 54 Cherry Hill Drive; the hearing was continued to May 28, 2026 after a wetland on the property was reclassified from Bordering Vegetated Wetland to Isolated Vegetated Wetland, requiring a new local waiver and updated documentation that staff had not yet received.

Public hearing scheduled on a Site Plan Review application for a new building addition and parking lot expansion at an Industrial II (I-2) zoned property on Cherry Hill Drive. No square footage, building use details, or dollar values are stated in the agenda.

Abiomed, Inc. is seeking site plan review for a new building addition and parking lot expansion at its 54 Cherry Hill Drive facility, located in the Industrial II (I-2) zoning district. The agenda does not specify the addition's size or square footage.

Special permit, waiver, and site plan review application for a mixed-use redevelopment of two parcels in the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District (Limited Mixed Use Sub-District). The proposal includes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units, 12 townhouse units, and retention of an existing single-family house, totaling 30 new residential units. Numerous design, parking, and circulation special permits and waivers are requested.

Public hearing on a mixed-use redevelopment of two adjacent parcels (Industrial I and Residential I underlying zoning) within the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District. The proposal includes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units, 12 townhouse units, and retention of an existing single-family house with associated site modifications. The applicant requested a continuance to the May 26, 2026 Planning Board meeting; multiple special permits, design waivers, and site plan review are required.

Applicant seeks Special Permits, Waivers, and Site Plan Review for a mixed-use redevelopment of two abutting parcels in the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District (Limited Mixed Use Sub-District). The project proposes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units and 12 townhouse units, plus retention of an existing single-family house. The applicant has requested a continuance to the June 9, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

PMZ Realty Trust is seeking special permits, waivers, and site plan review for a mixed-use redevelopment of two abutting parcels in the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District (Limited Mixed Use Sub-District). The proposal includes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units, 12 townhouse units, and retention of an existing single-family house, along with associated site modifications. The applicant requested a continuance to the June 23, 2026 Planning Board meeting.
The Planning Board is scheduled to hold a discussion on the town's Housing Production Plan (HPP). No further detail, scope, or action item is described in the agenda.
The Planning Board will discuss the town's Housing Production Plan (HPP). No project details, scope, or participants are identified in the agenda line item.

stories.name: 37 Popes Lane / 85 Newbury Street ANR Lot Combination
stories.address: 37 Popes Lane and 85 Newbury Street, Danvers, MA

events.summary: T.A.K.J., LLC is requesting Planning Board endorsement of an Approval Not Required (ANR) plan to eliminate the lot line between 37 Popes Lane (Map 48, Lot 28) and 85 Newbury Street (Map 48, Lot 28B), merging them into a single parcel. This lot consolidation appears related to the same applicant's concurrent site plan review request for a new building on the combined parcels (item 4).

T.A.K.J., LLC is seeking site plan review approval to construct a new building and associated site modifications at 37 Popes Lane and 85 Newbury Street, within the Highway Corridor Zone (HC2). The agenda provides no details on building type, use, or size.

New England Home for the Deaf and Winn Development Company request a modification to Special Permit Docket #23-5012, replacing a perpetual Danvers resident leasing preference (Condition 5) with a preference limited to the initial lease-up period, in order to conform with Fair Housing guidelines and HLC approval requirements. No change to the physical project is described.

The ZBA is considering a modification to Special Permit #23-5012 for the project at 154 and 170 Water Street. The applicants seek to replace the condition granting Danvers residents second preference 'in perpetuity' with a preference limited to the initial lease-up period only, citing Fair Housing Guidelines compliance.

stories.name: 8 Fairview Street Single-Family Home – Lot-Size Variance & Commissioner Appeal

events.summary: Applicant seeks a variance to build a new single-family home on a 10,000 sq ft lot in the R-3 zone where 30,000 sq ft is required, representing a 67% shortfall. The application also includes an appeal of Building Commissioner permit denial R-26-72 related to the same construction.

stories.name: 34 Endicott Street Accessory Building – Industrial Zone Setback Finding

events.summary: Owner of a Danversport-Industrial zoned parcel seeks Findings under Section 3.11 to construct an accessory building within 6.9 ft of the Endicott Street frontage and 7.1 ft of the Appleton Street frontage, where 30 ft is required on both. No details on the building's size or use are provided in the agenda.

stories.name: Pole Petition No. 20260113-1 – Maple Street Utility Pole Replacement
stories.address: Maple Street near 395 Maple Street, Danvers, MA

events.summary: Public hearing on a joint Danvers Electric and Verizon petition to replace one utility pole with associated wires, cable, anchors, guys, and fixtures on Maple Street near house #395 (pole 4-87-1). Scope is limited to a single pole replacement.

Notice of Intent filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1450) for work at 86 Elliott Street by individual applicant Majlinda Vaka. No project scope or description is provided in the agenda.

The Commission continued the Notice of Intent for new residential construction at 86 Elliott Street to April 23, 2026, pending a required site visit. The homeowners began clearing and foundation work under a valid building permit before discovering the site's proximity to an intermittent stream; work stopped immediately upon notification and a stormwater plan with permeable driveway and infiltration system has been submitted.

Notice of Intent filed by Majlinda Vaka for work at 86 Elliott Street under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1450). No project scope, unit count, or description provided in the agenda.

Notice of Intent under the Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1450) filed by Majlinda Vaka for undisclosed work at 86 Elliott Street. The applicant requested continuation to May 14, 2026; Commission discussion raised questions about disturbance calculations and stormwater flow patterns, with staff to provide a DEP guidance memo before the next hearing. Project type and scale are not described in the minutes.

Notice of Intent filed under 310 CMR 10.05(4) for work at 86 Elliott Street (DEP File No. 14-1450). Applicant is an individual, suggesting a residential context. Scope and nature of the proposed work are not described in the agenda.

An Enforcement Order is on the agenda for the same property and owner as a concurrent Notice of Intent (DEP File No. 14-1450), suggesting possible unpermitted wetlands activity at 86 Elliott Street. Nature of the alleged violation is not described in the agenda.

A 900 sq ft two-bedroom ADU (garage below, living unit above) was constructed at 86 Elliott Street without a Conservation permit; the Commission continued the hearing to May 28, 2026 after finding the structure intrudes into both the 35-ft no-disturb zone (2,287 sq ft impact) and 50-ft no-build zone (3,296 sq ft impact) along an intermittent stream. The applicant agreed to revise plans to shift the building and driveway further from the stream.

Majlinda Vaka has filed a Notice of Intent (DEP File No. 14-1450) for work at 86 Elliott Street within wetland resource areas. The agenda does not describe the scope of work; notably, a simultaneous Enforcement Order against the same owner at the same address is also on the agenda, suggesting an active or prior wetlands compliance issue.

The Conservation Commission will hear an Enforcement Order against property owner Majlinda Vaka at 86 Elliott Street, indicating an alleged or confirmed violation of the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act. An NOI (DEP File No. 14-1450) from the same owner at the same address is concurrently on the agenda.

Public hearing scheduled on a request for Design Special Permits (build-to-zone, side yard setback, street-facing transparency on High Street and Healy Court) and a Waiver for outdoor amenity space at 103 High Street in the High Street Mixed Use Corridor district. The applicant proposes a building expansion and associated site modifications; no unit counts or square footage are stated in the agenda.

The Affordable Housing Committee is reviewing revisions to the town's Housing Production Plan and will bring the updated plan to the Select Board for formal adoption once finalized. No detail on the scope of revisions or timeline beyond the March 18 committee meeting was provided.
The Danvers Affordable Housing Committee is actively working on a Housing Production Plan, which Select Board Member Bennett indicated is expected to come before the Select Board in June 2026.

The Select Board received a presentation from Land Use & Community Services Director Aaron Henry regarding a Housing Production Plan for Danvers. No unit targets, timelines, or policy details are described in the agenda.

DPW Director Stephen King and Land Use & Community Services Director Aaron Henry presented a Downtown update to the Select Board. No project scope, specific locations, or development details are described in the agenda.

Forest River Realty, LLC is seeking a Special Permit and Site Plan Review to construct a contractor's yard with associated site modifications at 13 Popes Lane in the Highway Corridor Zone. Approval is required under the Table of Allowable Uses and the site plan review provisions of the Zoning Bylaw.

Notice of Intent filed under 310 CMR 10.05(4) for work at 13 Popes Lane by a realty entity (DEP File No. 14-1451). Best-guess type is residential given the applicant name, but scope and nature of the proposed work are not described in the agenda.

Forest River Realty received an Order of Conditions to convert an existing two-family residence into a contractor yard at 13 Popes Lane. The project demolishes the older portion of the house, retains a newer addition as an office, converts paved areas to crushed stone, and adds 15 parking spaces, a bulk storage area, and a dumpster pad, with approximately 167 sq ft of wetland buffer impact; two parking spaces must be relocated out of the buffer zone before final filing.

Request for Certificate of Compliance for previously permitted work at 80 Andover Street (DEP File No. 14-0516), indicating a prior Order of Conditions is nearing close-out. Applicant is Seaside Legal Solutions; property owner is John Kiley, Jr. Original project scope is not described in the agenda.

Sign Design Inc. seeks a variance under Section 37.3 to allow aggregate signage of 249 square feet at a C-3 zoned commercial property, where 234 square feet is the by-right maximum. The 15-square-foot overage requires ZBA approval. No building or unit changes are involved.

stories.name: South Essex Sewerage District Capital Improvement Program

events.summary: SESD presented to the Select Board outlining approximately $390 million in priority capital projects to replace aging wastewater treatment infrastructure, including plant modernization and major pipeline replacements such as the Beverly–Danvers harbor crossing. SESD is seeking special state legislation granting relief from Prop 2½ limits and borrowing authority; the Board expressed concern about ratepayer impact, demanded community-specific cost projections and explicit guardrails, and asked SESD to return with refined legislative language before the town offers formal support.

stories.name: National Grid Utility Project — Conant, Berry, Alden, and Park Streets
stories.address: Conant Street, Berry Street, Alden Street, Park Street, Danvers, MA

events.summary: National Grid has begun a 12-week utility project affecting Conant, Berry, Alden, and Park Streets in Danvers, involving both overnight and daytime work with police details on site.

stories.name: Essex Tech Acquisition of Waco Building

events.summary: Select Board Member Djoko attended an Essex Tech information session regarding the school's purchase of the Waco building in Danvers. Discussions centered on office-space needs, parking coordination on Preston Street, and improved winter sidewalk maintenance for students; the Board encouraged continued collaboration between the town, DPW, and Essex Tech.

The Danvers Preservation Commission has completed a revision to the town's demolition bylaw, designated as Article 14 at Town Meeting scheduled for May 18, 2026. The revision is intended to streamline the demolition-review process for property owners; Select Board Member Bennett expressed hope for Town Meeting approval.

stories.name: 35 Doty Avenue Wetlands Enforcement Order — DEP 14-0883

events.summary: Conservation Commission check-in on an outstanding Enforcement Order at 35 Doty Avenue (DEP #14-0883) involving Edward Doherty. No details on the nature of the violation or remediation status are provided in the agenda.

Enforcement order check-in for unpermitted work at 35 Doty Avenue (DEP #14-0883). Required corrective actions include removal of a 1,200 sq ft sports court and replacement with lawn, relocation of granite wetland-boundary markers, planting of 375 sq ft of upland shrubs, and installation of a 100 sq ft pollinator garden. Work is expected complete by June 6, 2026, after which a site visit will be scheduled and a Certificate of Compliance issued.

Request for a Finding under Section 3.10 to substitute a pre-existing non-conforming restaurant use with a recording studio at 20 Water Street. No footprint changes are proposed; the property remains in an R-1 zone.

ZBA unanimously approved a Finding under Section 3.10 of the Zoning Bylaw (Docket 26-5134) to substitute a pre-existing non-conforming restaurant use with a recording studio at 20 Water Street. No footprint changes are involved; the studio will operate by appointment only, 9 AM–8 PM, with no retail or food service. Approval was conditioned on hours of operation and sign lighting shutoff one hour after closing.

Continued check-in on a Special Permit application under Section 30.2.D to convert an existing circa-1900 single-family home into two dwelling units. No applicant name or outcome detail provided in the agenda.

ZBA held a check-in (Docket 24-5085) on a 2024-approved special permit for conversion of a circa-1900 single-family home into a two-unit duplex at 41 Hobart Street; the completed structure deviated from approved plans with two front doors, an altered roofline, and a side-by-side unit layout instead of the approved front-to-back configuration. The board reached consensus — without a formal vote — to allow the as-built configuration, citing multi-department miscommunication and construction nearly at the interior-finish stage. The board noted that future applicants must return to the ZBA when exterior changes are contemplated regardless of staff approvals.

stories.name: Temar Properties Mobile Home Park Expansion

events.summary: Notice of Intent filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act for a property at 96-98 Newbury Street (DEP File No. 14-1449). Applicant is Temar Properties, LLC; project scope and scale are not described in the agenda.

Notice of Intent hearing for expansion of an existing mobile home park off Route 1, adding approximately 24 new units with stormwater improvements (detention basins, forebays) and minor added pavement (~280 sq ft) within the 100-ft wetland buffer; roughly 1.5 units fall within the buffer zone. ZBA and Planning Board approvals were previously obtained. The commission continued the hearing to April 9, 2026 pending a site visit.

Notice of Intent filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1449) for work at 96-98 Newbury Street by Temar Properties, LLC. No project scope or description is provided in the agenda.

The Conservation Commission approved an Order of Conditions for two new mobile homes and expanded pavement at an existing mobile home park, with a $2,500 bond for debris cleanup. All proposed structures fall outside the 35-ft no-disturb zone; a waiver was granted for 280 sq ft of pavement within the 50-ft no-build zone to accommodate emergency vehicle turning radius. Vote was 4–1 on both the waiver and the Order of Conditions.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Article 15 favorably for the May 18, 2026 Annual Town Meeting. The $317,000 appropriation funds pavement management, ADA upgrades including wheelchair ramps, school parking lot restoration, and a new roadway condition evaluation cycle. DPW targets five to six miles of roadway improvements per year and is building a public-facing road condition dashboard.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Article 16 favorably for Town Meeting. The program, running since 2020, converts long-maintained private ways into accepted public streets; of roughly 70 remaining private streets, approximately 36 are candidates for future acceptance, with two to three expected to complete the process this year. Additional survey work has been added to confirm rights-of-way on older roads with unclear layouts.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $652,000 for improvements to school buildings, Endicott Park, the Peabody Institute Library, and other municipal facilities. Ongoing structural work at the Library Pavilion was noted, and long-term planning for the Engine 2 fire station continues.

stories.name: Grounds Improvements / Rail Trail Westward Extension (Article 18)

events.summary: Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $166,000 for grounds improvements including softball field upgrades, field aeration and fertilization, and natural resource area fencing. The appropriation also advances the westward extension of the rail trail, coordinated with the state, with full build-out expected around 2028.

stories.name: Endicott Street Pump Station Generator Replacement (Article 21)

events.summary: Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $400,000 to replace a 35-year-old emergency generator serving the Endicott Street pump station, the town's second-largest, required due to age and state-mandated backup power regulations. The existing generator cannot be repurposed elsewhere due to sizing and funding-source constraints.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $500,000 for ongoing rehabilitation of the water treatment plant and groundwater well systems, covering pumps, chemical feeders, control components, and sand filter media replacement. Discussion addressed PFAS elimination at the plant, a resolved cross-connection in Middleton, and improved chemical procurement procedures following prior supply issues.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Article 28, appropriating $621,503 in 3M/DuPont class-action PFAS settlement funds to advance treatment design for Wells 1 and 2. Prior grant funding has partially completed the design; the town is pursuing additional federal support to finish the full design.

stories.name: R3-to-Highway Corridor Rezoning for Hockey Rink

events.summary: Citizen petition by Broadway Capital to rezone a parcel from R3 to Highway Corridor to enable development of a hockey rink; applicant stated financing is contingent on rezoning approval and they are prepared to conduct environmental, traffic, and sound studies. Both the Planning Board (unanimous) and the Select Board (4-1) voted unfavorable, citing the breadth of allowable Highway Corridor uses beyond just a rink and the legal prohibition on conditional zoning in Massachusetts. The petition proceeds to Town Meeting as a citizen-initiated article.

stories.name: Home-Based Dog Care Special Permit Petition (Residential Zones)

events.summary: Citizen petition by Danvers resident Melissa Nardone to allow small, owner-occupied in-home dog care by special permit in residential districts. The Planning Board voted unanimously unfavorable and the Select Board voted 5-0 unfavorable on the as-written petition, which covers R1, R2, and R3 districts; the Board referred it back to the Planning Board for language refinement, expressing general support for creating a regulatory framework for the use.

Town-initiated bylaw that would require registration of vacant buildings, impose annual registration fees, and create significant daily fines for noncompliance, formalizing existing monitoring practices. Select Board voted 4-1 favorable to advance to Town Meeting; dissenting member and some residents raised concerns about property rights and completeness of the proposal.

Article adds a third pathway to the existing demolition delay bylaw allowing the Preservation Commission to designate a property historically significant while still permitting minor work that does not affect its historical character, without triggering a full public hearing. Select Board voted 5-0 favorable to advance to Town Meeting.

Town Meeting article would transfer $408,675 in previously collected 40R incentive payments and transportation mitigation fees into the Downtown Improvement Fund to support updated traffic studies, streetscape planning, and related downtown improvements. Select Board voted 5-0 favorable; board confirmed 40R funds are not restricted to affordable housing.

Annual $20,000 appropriation for river maintenance dredging; the River Committee has pursued grants to minimize the town's direct cost. Major dredging work is likely deferred beyond 2028 pending competitive state grant availability. Select Board voted 5-0 favorable.

stories.name: 249 Andover Street Rezoning – R-3 to Highway Corridor Zone (Ice Skating Rink)

events.summary: Public hearing scheduled on a citizen's petition to rezone 249 Andover Street from Residential III (R-3) to Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ). The petition was filed by Dana R. McNamee, Manager of Bay State RE Holdings, LLC, identified as the site property owner. No proposed development or project scope is described in the agenda.

Public hearing on a citizen's petition to rezone 249 Andover Street (Map 047, Parcel 015A) from Residential III (R-3) to Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ) to enable a community ice skating rink with a single sheet of ice, approximately 50 parking spaces, lobby, concessions, and locker rooms. The site has operated commercially for roughly 70 years (former animal clinic) and presents significant wetland and structural constraints limiting residential use; a 70-foot bridge over wetlands would be required for access. Board members raised concerns about abutter outreach, traffic safety near Route 1, environmental impacts, future HCZ uses if the rink fails, and available alternatives such as a nonconforming-use substitution through the ZBA; hearing continued to April 6, 2026.

Citizen's petition by Bay State RE Holdings, LLC to rezone 249 Andover Street from Residential III (R-3) to the Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ). No development program is described in the agenda; the rezoning would unlock commercial and mixed uses permitted in the HCZ district.

stories.name: Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Commercial Kennel/Boarding/Training Use in Residential Districts

events.summary: Public hearing scheduled on a citizen's petition to amend the Danvers Zoning Bylaw Table of Allowable Uses, reclassifying Commercial Kennel/Boarding/Training from a prohibited use to a use allowable by Special Permit from the ZBA in R-1, R-2, and R-3 residential districts. The petition was filed by Melissa Nardone. No specific property address or applicant business is identified.

Citizen's petition to amend Table 1 of the Danvers Zoning Bylaw to allow commercial kennel and boarding/training facilities by special permit from the ZBA in R-1, R-2, and R-3 residential districts, where they are currently prohibited.

stories.name: St. John's Preparatory School Dining Hall Improvements

events.summary: Request for Determination of Applicability filed by St. John Preparatory School for work at 65 Summer Street (DCC File No. 2026-02). Project scope and relationship to wetland resource areas are not described in the agenda.

Request for Determination of Applicability for minor site work tied to dining hall improvements, consisting of pavement resurfacing, curb replacement, and a net ~350 sq ft reduction of impervious surface within the 100-ft wetland buffer; no work proposed in the 0–50 ft zones. The commission issued a Negative Determination, clearing the conservation hurdle; the broader project includes demolition of a kitchen/back-of-house area and a new addition located outside conservation jurisdiction.

stories.name: Commercial Kennel / In-Home Dog Daycare Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Table of Allowable Uses

events.summary: Public hearing on a citizen's petition to amend Table 1 of the Danvers Zoning Bylaw to reclassify Commercial Kennel / Boarding / Training from a prohibited use to a use allowable by Special Permit from the ZBA in the R-1, R-2, and R-3 zoning districts, intended to legalize and regulate in-home dog daycare operations. Petitioner argues roughly 15–20 similar unregulated home daycares already exist in Danvers and that a Special Permit process would impose Board of Health and ZBA oversight. Board members and multiple public commenters raised concerns about petition language ambiguity, enforceability, noise, lot-size appropriateness, and potential for large-scale commercial operations in residential zones; hearing continued to April 6, 2026.

stories.name: 156 Maple Street – Special Permit / Site Plan Review

events.summary: Staff noted that a Special Permit and Site Plan hearing for 156 Maple Street is scheduled for the April 6, 2026 Planning Board meeting; no applicant, project description, use, or scale details were provided in these minutes.

The Select Board received a high-level overview of the draft Open Space and Recreation Plan, a comprehensive town-wide rewrite incorporating nearly 500 community comments focused on parks, trail connectivity, and accessibility. Maintaining an approved plan is required for state open-space grant eligibility; the plan will go to the Planning Board, Recreation Committee, and other committees before returning to the Select Board in 4–6 weeks, with a target state submission by end of May 2026.

stories.name: Wenham Street and Wellesley Road Water Main Replacement
stories.address: Wenham Street and Wellesley Road, Danvers, MA

events.summary: The Town Manager announced that the Wenham Street and Wellesley Road water main replacement project is scheduled to begin the week following the March 17, 2026 meeting, weather permitting. No contractor, cost, or scope details were provided in the minutes.

The Preservation Commission is scheduled to hold a required public hearing on proposed alterations to the historic Old Firehouse, which has been formally deemed historically significant and triggers a mandatory 45-day review period. No applicant, specific address, or description of the proposed alterations was included in the minutes.

The Select Board will receive a presentation on Open Space from Land Use & Community Services Director Aaron Henry. No specific properties, acreage, acquisitions, or funding amounts are identified in the agenda line item.
Joint Land Use Summit agenda item covering long-range planning activities for Danvers. No project specifics, scope, or timeline are provided in the agenda.
Joint Land Use Summit agenda item presenting a broad overview of all potential town meeting articles. No individual articles, subjects, or sponsors are identified in the agenda.

Discussion of proposed articles for the 2027 Special Town Meeting at a joint Land Use Summit of the Select Board, Planning Board, and ZBA. The content or number of articles is not specified in the agenda; the land use context suggests zoning or bylaw amendments are likely among them.
Agenda item featuring Kim Lundgren Associates presenting on or in connection with the Green Communities program. No project scope, grant amounts, or specific initiatives are described in the agenda.

Planning Board will present a status update on long-range planning activities at a joint Land Use Summit of the Select Board, Planning Board, and Zoning Board of Appeals. No project specifics or documents are included in the agenda.

Joint board discussion of all potential articles and proposed 2027 Special Town Meeting warrant articles. Content is unknown from the agenda alone but STM articles commonly include zoning bylaw amendments, capital items, and general bylaws.

Kim Lundgren Associates will present to the joint board session on the Green Communities program, which typically involves municipal energy-efficiency designation criteria, grant eligibility, or action-plan compliance. No project specifics are stated in the agenda.
The Planning Board will discuss the town's Housing Production Plan (HPP). No project details, scope, or participants are identified in the agenda line item.

events.project_name: Pole Petition No. 20260113-1 – Maple Street Utility Pole Replacement
events.address: Maple Street near 395 Maple Street, Danvers, MA
events.summary: Public hearing on a joint Danvers Electric and Verizon petition to replace one utility pole with associated wires, cable, anchors, guys, and fixtures on Maple Street near house #395 (pole 4-87-1). Scope is limited to a single pole replacement.

Majlinda Vaka has filed a Notice of Intent (DEP File No. 14-1450) for work at 86 Elliott Street within wetland resource areas. The agenda does not describe the scope of work; notably, a simultaneous Enforcement Order against the same owner at the same address is also on the agenda, suggesting an active or prior wetlands compliance issue.

The Conservation Commission will hear an Enforcement Order against property owner Majlinda Vaka at 86 Elliott Street, indicating an alleged or confirmed violation of the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act. An NOI (DEP File No. 14-1450) from the same owner at the same address is concurrently on the agenda.
The Planning Board is scheduled to hold a discussion on the town's Housing Production Plan (HPP). No further detail, scope, or action item is described in the agenda.

events.address: 156 and 158 Maple Street, Danvers, MA (Map 43, Lot 543 and Map 43, Lot 544)
events.applicant: Peter M. Zagorski, Trustee PMZ Realty Trust
events.summary: Applicant seeks Special Permits, Waivers, and Site Plan Review for a mixed-use redevelopment of two abutting parcels in the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District (Limited Mixed Use Sub-District). The project proposes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units and 12 townhouse units, plus retention of an existing single-family house. The applicant has requested a continuance to the June 9, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

Public hearing scheduled on a request for Design Special Permits (build-to-zone, side yard setback, street-facing transparency on High Street and Healy Court) and a Waiver for outdoor amenity space at 103 High Street in the High Street Mixed Use Corridor district. The applicant proposes a building expansion and associated site modifications; no unit counts or square footage are stated in the agenda.

events.address: 54 Cherry Hill Drive, Danvers, MA (Map 29, Lot 11)
events.summary: Public hearing scheduled on a Site Plan Review application for a new building addition and parking lot expansion at an Industrial II (I-2) zoned property on Cherry Hill Drive. No square footage, building use details, or dollar values are stated in the agenda.

DPW Director Stephen King and Land Use & Community Services Director Aaron Henry presented a Downtown update to the Select Board. No project scope, specific locations, or development details are described in the agenda.

The Select Board received a presentation from Land Use & Community Services Director Aaron Henry regarding a Housing Production Plan for Danvers. No unit targets, timelines, or policy details are described in the agenda.

Notice of Intent filed under 310 CMR 10.05(4) for work at 86 Elliott Street (DEP File No. 14-1450). Applicant is an individual, suggesting a residential context. Scope and nature of the proposed work are not described in the agenda.

An Enforcement Order is on the agenda for the same property and owner as a concurrent Notice of Intent (DEP File No. 14-1450), suggesting possible unpermitted wetlands activity at 86 Elliott Street. Nature of the alleged violation is not described in the agenda.

Request for Certificate of Compliance for previously permitted work at 80 Andover Street (DEP File No. 14-0516), indicating a prior Order of Conditions is nearing close-out. Applicant is Seaside Legal Solutions; property owner is John Kiley, Jr. Original project scope is not described in the agenda.

Notice of Intent filed under 310 CMR 10.05(4) for work at 13 Popes Lane by a realty entity (DEP File No. 14-1451). Best-guess type is residential given the applicant name, but scope and nature of the proposed work are not described in the agenda.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive Notice of Intent
events.summary: Notice of Intent filed under 310 CMR 10.05(4) for work at 54 Cherry Hill Drive by a corporate applicant, Abiomed, Inc. (DEP File No. 14-1452). Best-guess type is commercial given the corporate name, but scope and nature of the proposed work are not described in the agenda.

A 900 sq ft two-bedroom ADU (garage below, living unit above) was constructed at 86 Elliott Street without a Conservation permit; the Commission continued the hearing to May 28, 2026 after finding the structure intrudes into both the 35-ft no-disturb zone (2,287 sq ft impact) and 50-ft no-build zone (3,296 sq ft impact) along an intermittent stream. The applicant agreed to revise plans to shift the building and driveway further from the stream.

Forest River Realty received an Order of Conditions to convert an existing two-family residence into a contractor yard at 13 Popes Lane. The project demolishes the older portion of the house, retains a newer addition as an office, converts paved areas to crushed stone, and adds 15 parking spaces, a bulk storage area, and a dumpster pad, with approximately 167 sq ft of wetland buffer impact; two parking spaces must be relocated out of the buffer zone before final filing.

Abiomed, Inc. filed a Notice of Intent for minor site work and installation of a no-storage sign at 54 Cherry Hill Drive; the hearing was continued to May 28, 2026 after a wetland on the property was reclassified from Bordering Vegetated Wetland to Isolated Vegetated Wetland, requiring a new local waiver and updated documentation that staff had not yet received.

events.applicant: Peter M. Zagorski, Trustee PMZ Realty Trust
events.summary: Public hearing on a mixed-use redevelopment of two adjacent parcels (Industrial I and Residential I underlying zoning) within the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District. The proposal includes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units, 12 townhouse units, and retention of an existing single-family house with associated site modifications. The applicant requested a continuance to the May 26, 2026 Planning Board meeting; multiple special permits, design waivers, and site plan review are required.

Forest River Realty, LLC is seeking a Special Permit and Site Plan Review to construct a contractor's yard with associated site modifications at 13 Popes Lane in the Highway Corridor Zone. Approval is required under the Table of Allowable Uses and the site plan review provisions of the Zoning Bylaw.

Sign Design Inc. seeks a variance under Section 37.3 to allow aggregate signage of 249 square feet at a C-3 zoned commercial property, where 234 square feet is the by-right maximum. The 15-square-foot overage requires ZBA approval. No building or unit changes are involved.

events.applicant: New England Home for the Deaf / Winn Development Company
events.summary: New England Home for the Deaf and Winn Development Company request a modification to Special Permit Docket #23-5012, replacing a perpetual Danvers resident leasing preference (Condition 5) with a preference limited to the initial lease-up period, in order to conform with Fair Housing guidelines and HLC approval requirements. No change to the physical project is described.

events.project_name: South Essex Sewerage District Capital Improvement Program
events.summary: SESD presented to the Select Board outlining approximately $390 million in priority capital projects to replace aging wastewater treatment infrastructure, including plant modernization and major pipeline replacements such as the Beverly–Danvers harbor crossing. SESD is seeking special state legislation granting relief from Prop 2½ limits and borrowing authority; the Board expressed concern about ratepayer impact, demanded community-specific cost projections and explicit guardrails, and asked SESD to return with refined legislative language before the town offers formal support.

events.project_name: National Grid Utility Project — Conant, Berry, Alden, and Park Streets
events.address: Conant Street, Berry Street, Alden Street, Park Street, Danvers, MA
events.summary: National Grid has begun a 12-week utility project affecting Conant, Berry, Alden, and Park Streets in Danvers, involving both overnight and daytime work with police details on site.

events.project_name: Essex Tech Acquisition of Waco Building
events.summary: Select Board Member Djoko attended an Essex Tech information session regarding the school's purchase of the Waco building in Danvers. Discussions centered on office-space needs, parking coordination on Preston Street, and improved winter sidewalk maintenance for students; the Board encouraged continued collaboration between the town, DPW, and Essex Tech.

The Danvers Preservation Commission has completed a revision to the town's demolition bylaw, designated as Article 14 at Town Meeting scheduled for May 18, 2026. The revision is intended to streamline the demolition-review process for property owners; Select Board Member Bennett expressed hope for Town Meeting approval.
The Danvers Affordable Housing Committee is actively working on a Housing Production Plan, which Select Board Member Bennett indicated is expected to come before the Select Board in June 2026.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive Building Addition & Parking Expansion
events.address: 54 Cherry Hill Drive, Danvers, MA (Map 29, Lot 11)
events.summary: Planning Board public hearing on a site plan review application by Abiomed, Inc. for a new building addition, parking lot expansion, and associated site modifications at an Industrial II (I-2) zoned property. No square footage or dollar figures were stated in the agenda.

Notice of Intent filed by Majlinda Vaka for work at 86 Elliott Street under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1450). No project scope, unit count, or description provided in the agenda.

Conservation Commission check-in on an outstanding Enforcement Order at 35 Doty Avenue (DEP #14-0883) involving Edward Doherty. No details on the nature of the violation or remediation status are provided in the agenda.

events.project_name: 86 Elliott Street Notice of Intent — DEP 14-1450
events.summary: Notice of Intent under the Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1450) filed by Majlinda Vaka for undisclosed work at 86 Elliott Street. The applicant requested continuation to May 14, 2026; Commission discussion raised questions about disturbance calculations and stormwater flow patterns, with staff to provide a DEP guidance memo before the next hearing. Project type and scale are not described in the minutes.

events.project_name: 35 Doty Avenue Wetlands Enforcement Order — DEP 14-0883
events.summary: Enforcement order check-in for unpermitted work at 35 Doty Avenue (DEP #14-0883). Required corrective actions include removal of a 1,200 sq ft sports court and replacement with lawn, relocation of granite wetland-boundary markers, planting of 375 sq ft of upland shrubs, and installation of a 100 sq ft pollinator garden. Work is expected complete by June 6, 2026, after which a site visit will be scheduled and a Certificate of Compliance issued.

Continued check-in on a Special Permit application under Section 30.2.D to convert an existing circa-1900 single-family home into two dwelling units. No applicant name or outcome detail provided in the agenda.

events.address: 20 Water Street, Danvers, MA (R-1, Map 059 Lot 131)
events.summary: Request for a Finding under Section 3.10 to substitute a pre-existing non-conforming restaurant use with a recording studio at 20 Water Street. No footprint changes are proposed; the property remains in an R-1 zone.

ZBA unanimously approved a Finding under Section 3.10 of the Zoning Bylaw (Docket 26-5134) to substitute a pre-existing non-conforming restaurant use with a recording studio at 20 Water Street. No footprint changes are involved; the studio will operate by appointment only, 9 AM–8 PM, with no retail or food service. Approval was conditioned on hours of operation and sign lighting shutoff one hour after closing.

ZBA held a check-in (Docket 24-5085) on a 2024-approved special permit for conversion of a circa-1900 single-family home into a two-unit duplex at 41 Hobart Street; the completed structure deviated from approved plans with two front doors, an altered roofline, and a side-by-side unit layout instead of the approved front-to-back configuration. The board reached consensus — without a formal vote — to allow the as-built configuration, citing multi-department miscommunication and construction nearly at the interior-finish stage. The board noted that future applicants must return to the ZBA when exterior changes are contemplated regardless of staff approvals.

Notice of Intent filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1449) for work at 96-98 Newbury Street by Temar Properties, LLC. No project scope or description is provided in the agenda.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive Request for Determination of Applicability
events.summary: Request for Determination of Applicability filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DCC File No. 2026-01) by Abiomed for work at 54 Cherry Hill Drive. The agenda provides no description of the proposed work; outcome of the determination is not yet known.

Notice of Intent filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act (DEP File No. 14-1450) for work at 86 Elliott Street by individual applicant Majlinda Vaka. No project scope or description is provided in the agenda.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Article 15 favorably for the May 18, 2026 Annual Town Meeting. The $317,000 appropriation funds pavement management, ADA upgrades including wheelchair ramps, school parking lot restoration, and a new roadway condition evaluation cycle. DPW targets five to six miles of roadway improvements per year and is building a public-facing road condition dashboard.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Article 16 favorably for Town Meeting. The program, running since 2020, converts long-maintained private ways into accepted public streets; of roughly 70 remaining private streets, approximately 36 are candidates for future acceptance, with two to three expected to complete the process this year. Additional survey work has been added to confirm rights-of-way on older roads with unclear layouts.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $652,000 for improvements to school buildings, Endicott Park, the Peabody Institute Library, and other municipal facilities. Ongoing structural work at the Library Pavilion was noted, and long-term planning for the Engine 2 fire station continues.

events.project_name: Grounds Improvements / Rail Trail Westward Extension (Article 18)
events.summary: Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $166,000 for grounds improvements including softball field upgrades, field aeration and fertilization, and natural resource area fencing. The appropriation also advances the westward extension of the rail trail, coordinated with the state, with full build-out expected around 2028.

events.project_name: Endicott Street Pump Station Generator Replacement (Article 21)
events.summary: Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $400,000 to replace a 35-year-old emergency generator serving the Endicott Street pump station, the town's second-largest, required due to age and state-mandated backup power regulations. The existing generator cannot be repurposed elsewhere due to sizing and funding-source constraints.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend $500,000 for ongoing rehabilitation of the water treatment plant and groundwater well systems, covering pumps, chemical feeders, control components, and sand filter media replacement. Discussion addressed PFAS elimination at the plant, a resolved cross-connection in Middleton, and improved chemical procurement procedures following prior supply issues.

Select Board voted 5-0 to recommend Article 28, appropriating $621,503 in 3M/DuPont class-action PFAS settlement funds to advance treatment design for Wells 1 and 2. Prior grant funding has partially completed the design; the town is pursuing additional federal support to finish the full design.

The Conservation Commission approved an Order of Conditions for two new mobile homes and expanded pavement at an existing mobile home park, with a $2,500 bond for debris cleanup. All proposed structures fall outside the 35-ft no-disturb zone; a waiver was granted for 280 sq ft of pavement within the 50-ft no-build zone to accommodate emergency vehicle turning radius. Vote was 4–1 on both the waiver and the Order of Conditions.

events.project_name: Abiomed Office and Parking Expansion at 54 Cherry Hill Drive
events.summary: The Conservation Commission issued a unanimous positive Determination of Applicability for Abiomed's proposed office and parking expansion, requiring the company to refile as a full Notice of Intent before proceeding. The project involves approximately 9,000 sq ft of new pavement, roughly 267 total parking spaces (23–24 new spaces within wetland buffer zones), and a new two-story office building connected to the existing R&D/manufacturing facility; commissioners found the project scope too significant for the RDA process.

The Commission continued the Notice of Intent for new residential construction at 86 Elliott Street to April 23, 2026, pending a required site visit. The homeowners began clearing and foundation work under a valid building permit before discovering the site's proximity to an intermittent stream; work stopped immediately upon notification and a stormwater plan with permeable driveway and infiltration system has been submitted.

events.project_name: R3-to-Highway Corridor Rezoning for Hockey Rink
events.summary: Citizen petition by Broadway Capital to rezone a parcel from R3 to Highway Corridor to enable development of a hockey rink; applicant stated financing is contingent on rezoning approval and they are prepared to conduct environmental, traffic, and sound studies. Both the Planning Board (unanimous) and the Select Board (4-1) voted unfavorable, citing the breadth of allowable Highway Corridor uses beyond just a rink and the legal prohibition on conditional zoning in Massachusetts. The petition proceeds to Town Meeting as a citizen-initiated article.

events.project_name: Home-Based Dog Care Special Permit Petition (Residential Zones)
events.summary: Citizen petition by Danvers resident Melissa Nardone to allow small, owner-occupied in-home dog care by special permit in residential districts. The Planning Board voted unanimously unfavorable and the Select Board voted 5-0 unfavorable on the as-written petition, which covers R1, R2, and R3 districts; the Board referred it back to the Planning Board for language refinement, expressing general support for creating a regulatory framework for the use.

Town-initiated bylaw that would require registration of vacant buildings, impose annual registration fees, and create significant daily fines for noncompliance, formalizing existing monitoring practices. Select Board voted 4-1 favorable to advance to Town Meeting; dissenting member and some residents raised concerns about property rights and completeness of the proposal.

Article adds a third pathway to the existing demolition delay bylaw allowing the Preservation Commission to designate a property historically significant while still permitting minor work that does not affect its historical character, without triggering a full public hearing. Select Board voted 5-0 favorable to advance to Town Meeting.

Town Meeting article would transfer $408,675 in previously collected 40R incentive payments and transportation mitigation fees into the Downtown Improvement Fund to support updated traffic studies, streetscape planning, and related downtown improvements. Select Board voted 5-0 favorable; board confirmed 40R funds are not restricted to affordable housing.

Annual $20,000 appropriation for river maintenance dredging; the River Committee has pursued grants to minimize the town's direct cost. Major dredging work is likely deferred beyond 2028 pending competitive state grant availability. Select Board voted 5-0 favorable.

events.project_name: Zoning Map Amendment – 249 Andover Street (R-3 to HCZ)
events.applicant: Bay State RE Holdings, LLC (Dana R. MacNamee, Manager)
events.summary: Citizen's petition by Bay State RE Holdings, LLC to rezone 249 Andover Street from Residential III (R-3) to the Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ). No development program is described in the agenda; the rezoning would unlock commercial and mixed uses permitted in the HCZ district.

events.project_name: Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Commercial Kennel/Boarding Use in Residential Districts
events.summary: Citizen's petition to amend Table 1 of the Danvers Zoning Bylaw to allow commercial kennel and boarding/training facilities by special permit from the ZBA in R-1, R-2, and R-3 residential districts, where they are currently prohibited.

events.address: 156 Maple Street (Map 43, Lot 543) and 158 Maple Street (Map 43, Lot 544), Danvers, MA
events.applicant: PMZ Realty Trust (Peter M. Zagorski, Trustee)
events.summary: Special permit, waiver, and site plan review application for a mixed-use redevelopment of two parcels in the Maple Street Traditional Neighborhood Development Overlay District (Limited Mixed Use Sub-District). The proposal includes a mixed-use building with 18 residential units, 12 townhouse units, and retention of an existing single-family house, totaling 30 new residential units. Numerous design, parking, and circulation special permits and waivers are requested.

Notice of Intent filed under the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act for a property at 96-98 Newbury Street (DEP File No. 14-1449). Applicant is Temar Properties, LLC; project scope and scale are not described in the agenda.

events.project_name: 54 Cherry Hill Drive Request for Determination of Applicability
events.summary: Request for Determination of Applicability filed with the Danvers Conservation Commission for work at 54 Cherry Hill Drive (DCC File No. 2026-01). Applicant is Abiomed; project scope and relationship to wetland resource areas are not described in the agenda.

events.project_name: 65 Summer Street Request for Determination of Applicability
events.summary: Request for Determination of Applicability filed by St. John Preparatory School for work at 65 Summer Street (DCC File No. 2026-02). Project scope and relationship to wetland resource areas are not described in the agenda.

events.project_name: Temar Properties Mobile Home Park Expansion
events.summary: Notice of Intent hearing for expansion of an existing mobile home park off Route 1, adding approximately 24 new units with stormwater improvements (detention basins, forebays) and minor added pavement (~280 sq ft) within the 100-ft wetland buffer; roughly 1.5 units fall within the buffer zone. ZBA and Planning Board approvals were previously obtained. The commission continued the hearing to April 9, 2026 pending a site visit.

events.project_name: Abiomed R&D Facility Addition — 54 Cherry Hill Drive
events.summary: Request for Determination of Applicability for expansion of a 10.4-acre R&D facility, replacing approximately 8,000–9,000 sq ft of existing building with a ~23,000 sq ft addition, plus parking expansion, EV charging, ADA routes, and landscaping; net new impervious within the 100-ft buffer is approximately 9,180 sq ft. One commissioner expressed the view that project scale warrants a full Order of Conditions rather than a negative determination. Hearing continued to April 9, 2026 pending a site visit.

events.project_name: St. John's Preparatory School Dining Hall Improvements
events.summary: Request for Determination of Applicability for minor site work tied to dining hall improvements, consisting of pavement resurfacing, curb replacement, and a net ~350 sq ft reduction of impervious surface within the 100-ft wetland buffer; no work proposed in the 0–50 ft zones. The commission issued a Negative Determination, clearing the conservation hurdle; the broader project includes demolition of a kitchen/back-of-house area and a new addition located outside conservation jurisdiction.

events.project_name: Zoning Map Amendment – 249 Andover Street Rezoning (R-3 to HCZ)
events.summary: Public hearing scheduled on a citizen's petition to rezone 249 Andover Street from Residential III (R-3) to Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ). The petition was filed by Dana R. McNamee, Manager of Bay State RE Holdings, LLC, identified as the site property owner. No proposed development or project scope is described in the agenda.

events.project_name: Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Commercial Kennel/Boarding/Training Use in Residential Districts
events.summary: Public hearing scheduled on a citizen's petition to amend the Danvers Zoning Bylaw Table of Allowable Uses, reclassifying Commercial Kennel/Boarding/Training from a prohibited use to a use allowable by Special Permit from the ZBA in R-1, R-2, and R-3 residential districts. The petition was filed by Melissa Nardone. No specific property address or applicant business is identified.

events.project_name: 249 Andover Street Rezoning – R-3 to Highway Corridor Zone (Ice Skating Rink)
events.summary: Public hearing on a citizen's petition to rezone 249 Andover Street (Map 047, Parcel 015A) from Residential III (R-3) to Highway Corridor Zone (HCZ) to enable a community ice skating rink with a single sheet of ice, approximately 50 parking spaces, lobby, concessions, and locker rooms. The site has operated commercially for roughly 70 years (former animal clinic) and presents significant wetland and structural constraints limiting residential use; a 70-foot bridge over wetlands would be required for access. Board members raised concerns about abutter outreach, traffic safety near Route 1, environmental impacts, future HCZ uses if the rink fails, and available alternatives such as a nonconforming-use substitution through the ZBA; hearing continued to April 6, 2026.

events.project_name: Commercial Kennel / In-Home Dog Daycare Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Table of Allowable Uses
events.summary: Public hearing on a citizen's petition to amend Table 1 of the Danvers Zoning Bylaw to reclassify Commercial Kennel / Boarding / Training from a prohibited use to a use allowable by Special Permit from the ZBA in the R-1, R-2, and R-3 zoning districts, intended to legalize and regulate in-home dog daycare operations. Petitioner argues roughly 15–20 similar unregulated home daycares already exist in Danvers and that a Special Permit process would impose Board of Health and ZBA oversight. Board members and multiple public commenters raised concerns about petition language ambiguity, enforceability, noise, lot-size appropriateness, and potential for large-scale commercial operations in residential zones; hearing continued to April 6, 2026.

events.project_name: 156 Maple Street – Special Permit / Site Plan Review
events.summary: Staff noted that a Special Permit and Site Plan hearing for 156 Maple Street is scheduled for the April 6, 2026 Planning Board meeting; no applicant, project description, use, or scale details were provided in these minutes.

The Select Board will receive a presentation on Open Space from Land Use & Community Services Director Aaron Henry. No specific properties, acreage, acquisitions, or funding amounts are identified in the agenda line item.

job_contact.role: Director of Land Use and Community Services
job_contact.name: Aaron Henry

events.summary: The Select Board received a high-level overview of the draft Open Space and Recreation Plan, a comprehensive town-wide rewrite incorporating nearly 500 community comments focused on parks, trail connectivity, and accessibility. Maintaining an approved plan is required for state open-space grant eligibility; the plan will go to the Planning Board, Recreation Committee, and other committees before returning to the Select Board in 4–6 weeks, with a target state submission by end of May 2026.

events.project_name: Wenham Street and Wellesley Road Water Main Replacement
events.address: Wenham Street and Wellesley Road, Danvers, MA
events.summary: The Town Manager announced that the Wenham Street and Wellesley Road water main replacement project is scheduled to begin the week following the March 17, 2026 meeting, weather permitting. No contractor, cost, or scope details were provided in the minutes.

The Affordable Housing Committee is reviewing revisions to the town's Housing Production Plan and will bring the updated plan to the Select Board for formal adoption once finalized. No detail on the scope of revisions or timeline beyond the March 18 committee meeting was provided.

The Preservation Commission is scheduled to hold a required public hearing on proposed alterations to the historic Old Firehouse, which has been formally deemed historically significant and triggers a mandatory 45-day review period. No applicant, specific address, or description of the proposed alterations was included in the minutes.

upcoming.summary: Joint Land Use Summit agenda item covering long-range planning activities for Danvers. No project specifics, scope, or timeline are provided in the agenda.
Joint Land Use Summit agenda item presenting a broad overview of all potential town meeting articles. No individual articles, subjects, or sponsors are identified in the agenda.

Discussion of proposed articles for the 2027 Special Town Meeting at a joint Land Use Summit of the Select Board, Planning Board, and ZBA. The content or number of articles is not specified in the agenda; the land use context suggests zoning or bylaw amendments are likely among them.
Agenda item featuring Kim Lundgren Associates presenting on or in connection with the Green Communities program. No project scope, grant amounts, or specific initiatives are described in the agenda.

Planning Board will present a status update on long-range planning activities at a joint Land Use Summit of the Select Board, Planning Board, and Zoning Board of Appeals. No project specifics or documents are included in the agenda.

Joint board discussion of all potential articles and proposed 2027 Special Town Meeting warrant articles. Content is unknown from the agenda alone but STM articles commonly include zoning bylaw amendments, capital items, and general bylaws.

Kim Lundgren Associates will present to the joint board session on the Green Communities program, which typically involves municipal energy-efficiency designation criteria, grant eligibility, or action-plan compliance. No project specifics are stated in the agenda.
